name: Grade 1 Art & Creativity — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.title: Practice visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: National Core Arts Standards
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Art & Creativity pathway.

units.title: Investigate: visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to visual storytelling, craftsmanship, exhibit planning, and reflection.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
